div.div: Effect: The four kings are placed in four separate spots throughout the deck — yet after one cut, they're all found together. Method: before performing, hide three other face cards face-down behind three of the kings. Fan all four kings openly (the hidden cards aren't visible from the front). Place the visible kings at four positions in the deck — you're actually placing the hidden indifferent cards too. The fourth king goes on top. When the deck is cut, that top king falls to join the other three (with their hidden cards). Fan the deck to show four kings together.

The Four Kings is one of the oldest and most widely taught card tricks in the world, appearing in magic books from the 17th century. It teaches the most fundamental principle in card magic: that appearance and reality can be completely separated through misdirection.

The four kings are shown and placed separately throughout the deck. After a single cut, all four kings are found together. The secret: the other three kings have three indifferent court cards hidden between them; the fourth is placed last. After the cut, the hidden cards fill the apparent gap between the kings.

svg.text: Source: The Magician's Own Book (1862) · Heritage Parlor
